name: Stuffed Boiled Jumbo Artichokes
recipeIngredient: 2 bunches parsley finely chopped to yield 1/2 cup
1/2 cup fresh bread crumbs lightly toasted under broiler
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 6 quarts of water to a boil and add 1 tablespoon salt.
2. Pull off the outer dark green leaves of each artichoke until what remains is pale at the base and green at the ends of tips. Cut off the green points to about 1-inch from top of pointy end. Each time you cut the artichoke, rub the freshly cut sides with half a lemon. This will retain its light color.
3. Cut the artichokes into quarters. Using a small sharp knife, trim away the choke from the inside. Rub all cut edges with lemon.
4. Boil 5 minutes and refresh in cold water. Drain and set aside.
5. Preheat oven to 400 degrees. In a medium saute pan, saute onion and garlic over medium heat in 3 ounces of olive oil until softened, 8 to 10 minutes. Allow to cool and stir in parsley, grated cheese, and fresh toasted bread crumbs. Season with salt and pepper.
6. Place artichokes in a shallow casserole dish large enough to hold them in one layer. Bake uncovered about 20 minutes or until breadcrumbs are golden brown.
Yield: 4 as antipasto
